generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance FirstAxisMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor actuator component of the MABI MAX 100's first axis, interfacing with gearbox and friction elements in closed-loop control environment"
]

instance FirstAxisGearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearbox actuator component of the MABI MAX 100's first axis, transmitting torque from motor to joint with friction characteristics"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance FirstAxisMotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft of MABI MAX 100's first axis, providing high-resolution angular position feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FirstAxisMotorPositionData
]

instance FirstAxisSecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox of MABI MAX 100's first axis, providing redundant positional feedback for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FirstAxisGearboxPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance FirstAxisMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisMotorEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series position data streamed via MQTT from motor encoder of MABI MAX 100's first axis"
]

instance FirstAxisGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisSecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series position data streamed via MQTT from secondary encoder behind gearbox of MABI MAX 100's first axis"
]

instance FirstAxisActuatorParametersData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisMotorActuator, FirstAxisGearboxActuator
    base:desc "Operational parameters (torque, speed, current) from first axis actuators streamed via MQTT from Siemens SINUMERIK controller"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GeometricModelOfFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based geometric model of MABI MAX 100's first axis components (motor, gearbox, joints) for spatial relationship analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance FrictionBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable friction model for MABI MAX 100's first axis implemented in MathWorks Simulink, capturing Coulomb, viscous, and Stribeck friction effects"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance ComponentConnectorArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture model describing component-connector relationships for first axis actuators and sensors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcTool
]

instance FirstAxisOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated operational data traces from first axis including position, velocity, torque, and environmental parameters"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FirstAxisMotorPositionData, FirstAxisGearboxPositionData, FirstAxisActuatorParametersData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline
]

instance HistoricalMaintenanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical maintenance logs and environmental condition records for first axis friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationFramework
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling environment for structural and behavioral architecture description of MABI MAX 100's first axis"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowDataAggregationService, SystemArchitectureValidationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment executing friction behavioral models for first axis with real-time data integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MontiArcTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture description tool modeling component-connector relationships and state machines for first axis"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentInteractionAnalysisService
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software pipeline processing raw MQTT streams into aggregated operational data traces with validation checks"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, DataQualityAssuranceService
]

instance DataIntegrationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interoperability framework integrating heterogeneous data sources (sensor streams, historical records) for digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataConsolidationService
]

instance MQTTCommunicationProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight publish-subscribe protocol enabling real-time bidirectional communication between physical robot and digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataStreamingService
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIKController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time control interface providing low-level actuator data and command execution for MABI MAX 100's first axis"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlDataInterfaceService
]

// C6: Services
instance DigitalShadowDataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated one-way data collection service aggregating contextual traces from first axis sensors and actuators"
    DTDFVocab:provides FirstAxisFrictionCharacterizationInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service processing raw MQTT streams into validated operational data traces for first axis"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessedOperationalDataInsight
]

instance DataQualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validation and consistency checking service ensuring reliable digital representation of first axis data"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityMetricsInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing friction models using Simulink to analyze first axis dynamics with real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationRecommendationAction
]

instance ComponentInteractionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing component-connector relationships and state transitions in first axis architecture"
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractionCriticalityInsight
]

instance SystemArchitectureValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating SysML-based architecture models against operational requirements"
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureComplianceInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing MQTT-based real-time data transmission between physical and virtual components"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataStreamingPerformanceInsight
]

instance ControlDataInterfaceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing bidirectional interface between Siemens SINUMERIK controller and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControllerInterfaceStatusInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FirstAxisFrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Characterization of friction dynamics (Coulomb, viscous, Stribeck effects) for MABI MAX 100's first axis based on aggregated data"
]

instance ProcessedOperationalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validated and processed operational data traces ready for analysis and visualization"
]

instance DataQualityMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Metrics and validation results ensuring data quality and consistency for first axis digital representation"
]

instance FrictionCompensationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically generated compensation strategies for friction effects in first axis control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InteractionCriticalityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of critical component interactions and potential failure modes in first axis architecture"
]

instance ArchitectureComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results confirming SysML architecture models meet operational requirements"
]

instance DataStreamingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance metrics for MQTT-based real-time data streaming between physical and virtual components"
]

instance ControllerInterfaceStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status and operational metrics of the Siemens SINUMERIK controller interface"
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "High-precision motion control environment for MABI MAX 100 serial robot in manufacturing automation"
    base:contains FirstAxisMotorActuator, FirstAxisGearboxActuator,
                  FirstAxisMotorEncoder, FirstAxisSecondaryEncoder,
                  SiemensSINUMERIKController
]

// C5: Components (additional structural elements)
instance FirstAxisFrictionModelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Executable friction model component implemented in Simulink for first axis analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance FirstAxisDataAggregatorComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Software component aggregating and validating first axis sensor and actuator data streams"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationService, DataQualityAssuranceService
]

instance FirstAxisArchitectureModelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "SysML and MontiArc model components describing first axis architecture and behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SystemArchitectureValidationService, ComponentInteractionAnalysisService
]

// Added missing service for DataConsolidationService
instance DataConsolidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service consolidating heterogeneous data sources into unified digital shadow representation"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalMaintenanceDataInsight
]

instance HistoricalMaintenanceDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Consolidated historical maintenance and environmental data for friction analysis"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks, leveraging **multi-layered abstraction models** (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical architectures) to ensure **system-wide coordination**. **Architecture patterns** are defined using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which specify **component integration** via **interfaces, connectors, and composition rules**. **Behavioral modeling** is enhanced by integrating **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation environments) or **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., C++, Java) for **dynamic system behavior**. **Resource management** is implied through **heterogeneous stakeholder integration**, requiring **cross-tool and cross-notation consistency** (e.g., combining SysML with simulation models). The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) further suggests **contextual data aggregation** as a foundational layer for orchestration, but no explicit **resource allocation** or **scheduling mechanisms** are detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **model fidelity** through **multi-layered abstraction** (requirements → functions → logics → physics) and **validation/verification (V&V) methods** aligned with MBSE practices. **Uncertainty sources** include:
  - **Data inaccuracies**: Stemming from sensor limitations (e.g., resolution, calibration drift) or incomplete contextual traces in the Digital Shadow.
  - **Model approximations**: Arising from simplified behavioral or analytical representations (e.g., state machines vs. full physics-based simulations).
  - **Stakeholder interpretation gaps**: Due to heterogeneous perspectives in integrated models (e.g., SysML for architecture vs. Simulink for dynamics).

  **Error detection and correction** mechanisms are implicit in the **active components** of the DT, which process Digital Shadow data to identify discrepancies (e.g., via anomaly detection in simulation outputs). **Quality assurance** processes involve:
  - **Cross-tool validation**: Ensuring consistency between models (e.g., SysML IBDs vs. MontiArc Automaton) via formal or semi-formal methods.
  - **Simulation-based verification**: Using tools like **Simulink** to validate behavioral models against real-world data traces.
  - **Traceability links**: Maintaining bidirectional relationships between abstract models (e.g., functional requirements) and concrete implementations (e.g., C++ code) to isolate and rectify errors.
  The system’s **fidelity** is further constrained by the **passive nature of the Digital Shadow**, which lacks real-time bidirectional feedback, necessitating supplementary active components (e.g., control loops, adaptive algorithms) to mitigate validity gaps."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital shadow reference model explicitly mentions the use of **MQTT** as the communication protocol for streaming sensor data from physical objects to their digital counterparts. However, the document does not provide details on **network topology/architecture**, **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability/redundancy mechanisms**, or **security protocols** for network communication. The absence of explicit control flows (e.g., bidirectional interactions) further limits the scope of technical connection details."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The provided context does not explicitly mention **industry standards** or **certification frameworks** for Digital Twin (DT) systems. However, the use of **SysML** (ISO/IEC 19505) and **ADLs** (e.g., **AADL**, **ACME**) suggests adherence to **modeling and systems engineering standards**, though specific compliance requirements (e.g., ISO 23247 for DTs, IEC 62443 for cybersecurity) are not referenced. **Interoperability standards** are implied by the integration of heterogeneous tools (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++/Java), but no explicit protocols (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) or data exchange formats (e.g., JSON-LD, RDF) are cited. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) focuses on data aggregation but does not address standardization for DT-specific compliance or certification."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical physical constraints** via a **safety cell** in robotic applications (e.g., MABI robotic arm), which restricts the maximum working range to mitigate collision risks. **Cybersecurity protocols** are implied through the use of **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for real-time data streaming between physical and virtual components, though specific access control mechanisms (e.g., RBAC, encryption standards) are not detailed in the provided documents. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [47], [50]) is referenced as a structural enabler for interoperability and asset management but does not explicitly outline threat mitigation strategies or compliance with safety standards (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 61508). No explicit mention of **fail-safe mechanisms**, **risk assessment methodologies**, or **authentication systems** (e.g., OAuth, X.509) is provided. The **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]) is cited in the context of simulation model exchange but is unrelated to security or safety considerations in the given documents."
]
